decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. The current value is at the upper bound (500.0), but the editable variable definition allows tuning within [20.0, 500.0]. Since it is already at the maximum allowed by the schema bounds, no material increase is possible. Instead, I will increase the spreader width to enhance lateral heat spreading area, which is the next most effective lever.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader width maximizes the lateral area available for heat dissipation towards the Dirichlet boundaries, helping to lower the peak chip temperature further.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the lateral direction
Lower peak temperature in the chip component
More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader
risk_notes: The spreader width is approaching the design domain limit; ensure it does not exceed the domain width of 1.2m or overlap incorrectly with other components if geometry changes in future steps.
Diminishing returns may occur as the spreader approaches the full width of the cooling boundaries.
